# Objectives

To utilize the IIC-OSIC-TOOLS, an open-source verification suite, as the foundation for a complete FPGA verification workflow.

To develop a modular and reusable testbench using cocotb, written in Python, for verifying the functionality of a Verilog-based LED driver module.

To perform simulation-based verification using Icarus Verilog, integrated with cocotb, to ensure the correctness of the digital design.

To synthesize the verified design using the GateMate toolchain and deploy it onto the GateMate FPGA Evaluation Board.

To document the verification process, highlight the effectiveness of open-source tools, and provide a repeatable methodology for similar verification tasks.
